title: NOC 51112 – Technical Writers | GTR Immigration
description: Technical Writers under NOC 51112 are professional communication specialists responsible for creating clear, structured documentation for technical products, systems, and services across Canada. Classified under TEER 1, this occupation usually requires a university degree in a specialized technical field. Technical Writers are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group51 – Professional occupations in art and cultureSub-Major Group511 – Professional occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5111 – Writing, translating and related communications professionalsNOC Code51112

duties: Technical Writers perform some or all of the following duties:
Analyze technical material such as specifications, notes, and drawings
Conduct interviews and research to gather specialized information
Write, review, and update manuals, user guides, and documentation
Develop help systems and online documentation
Create installation, operation, and maintenance instructions
Develop on-screen instructions and error messages for software products
employment_requirements: A university degree in the area of specialization, such as computer science, engineering, life sciences, or a related field is usually required
Strong written communication skills are essential
Industry experience may be required for specialized fields such as medical or scientific writing
salary_prospects: $36,600 – $90,700 per year,depending on province, specialization, and industry sector.
With 23,100 projected job openings compared to 19,300 job seekers, NOC 51112 reflects strong employment prospects in Canada.
Rapid growth in technology and software industries
Expansion of digital products and services
Rising demand for user experience (UX) documentation
Major opportunities are concentrated in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, and Quebec.
